steps.title: Calculate distance traveled at constant speed
steps.content:
Bill first drove 22.0 miles, then drove at a constant speed of 70.0 miles per hour for 2 hours. Use the relationship distance = rate × time to find the distance traveled during the second segment:
$$\text{Distance} = 70.0 \text{ mph} \times 2 \text{ hours} = 140 \text{ miles}$$

steps.title: Add both segments to find total distance
steps.content:
The total distance Bill traveled is the sum of the initial 22.0 miles and the 140 miles traveled at constant speed:
$$\text{Total Distance} = 22.0 + 140 = 162 \text{ miles}$$
